387	open CategoryTheory Module
388	
389	instance (M : (Skeleton <| SemimoduleCat.{u} R)ˣ) : Module.Invertible R M :=
390	  .right (Quotient.eq.mp M.inv_mul).some.toLinearEquivₛ
391	
392	instance (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (M : (Skeleton <| ModuleCat.{u} R)ˣ) : Module.Invertible R M :=
393	  .right (Quotient.eq.mp M.inv_mul).some.toLinearEquiv
394	
395	instance : Small.{u} (Skeleton <| SemimoduleCat.{u} R)ˣ :=
396	  let sf := Σ n, ModuleCon R (Fin n → R)
397	  have {c₁ c₂ : sf} : c₁ = c₂ → c₁.2.Quotient ≃ₗ[R] c₂.2.Quotient := by rintro rfl; exact .refl ..
398	  let f (M : (Skeleton <| SemimoduleCat.{u} R)ˣ) : sf := ⟨_, Finite.kerReprₛ R M⟩
399	  small_of_injective (f := f) fun M N eq ↦ Units.ext <| Quotient.out_equiv_out.mp
400	    ⟨((Finite.reprEquivₛ R M).symm ≪≫ₗ this eq ≪≫ₗ Finite.reprEquivₛ R N).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
401	
402	instance (R : Type u) [CommRing R] : Small.{u} (Skeleton <| ModuleCat.{u} R)ˣ :=
403	  small_map (Units.mapEquiv <| Skeleton.mulEquiv ModuleCat.equivalenceSemimoduleCat).toEquiv
404	
405	/-- The Picard group of a commutative semiring R consists of the invertible R-modules,
406	up to isomorphism. -/
407	def CommRing.Pic (R : Type u) [CommSemiring R] : Type u :=
408	  Shrink (Skeleton <| SemimoduleCat.{u} R)ˣ
409	
410	open CommRing (Pic)
411	
412	noncomputable instance : CommGroup (Pic R) := fast_instance% (equivShrink _).symm.commGroup
413	
414	variable (M N : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]
415	  [Module.Invertible R M] [Module.Invertible R N]
416	
417	instance : Module.Invertible R (Finite.reprₛ R M) := .congr (Finite.reprEquivₛ R M).symm
418	
419	namespace CommRing.Pic
420	
421	variable {R} in
422	/-- A representative of an element in the Picard group. -/
423	abbrev AsModule (M : Pic R) : Type u := ((equivShrink _).symm M).val
424	
425	noncomputable instance : CoeSort (Pic R) (Type u) := ⟨AsModule⟩
426	
427	noncomputable instance (R) [CommRing R] (M : Pic R) : AddCommGroup M :=
428	  Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R
429	
430	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
431	private noncomputable def equivShrinkLinearEquiv (M : (Skeleton <| SemimoduleCat.{u} R)ˣ) :
432	    (id <| equivShrink _ M : Pic R) ≃ₗ[R] M :=
433	  have {M N : Skeleton (SemimoduleCat.{u} R)} : M = N → M ≃ₗ[R] N := by rintro rfl; exact .refl ..
434	  this (by simp)
435	
436	/-- The class of an invertible module in the Picard group. -/
437	protected noncomputable def mk : Pic R := equivShrink _ <|
438	  letI M' := Finite.reprₛ R M
439	  .mkOfMulEqOne ⟦.of R M'⟧ ⟦.of R (Dual R M')⟧ <| by
440	    rw [← toSkeleton, ← toSkeleton, mul_comm, ← Skeleton.toSkeleton_tensorObj]
441	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨(Invertible.linearEquiv R _).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
442	
443	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
444	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
445	/-- `mk R M` is indeed the class of `M`. -/
446	noncomputable def mk.linearEquiv : Pic.mk R M ≃ₗ[R] M :=
447	  equivShrinkLinearEquiv R _ ≪≫ₗ (Quotient.mk_out (s := isIsomorphicSetoid _)
448	    (SemimoduleCat.of R (Finite.reprₛ R M))).some.toLinearEquivₛ ≪≫ₗ Finite.reprEquivₛ R M
449	
450	variable {R M N}
451	
452	theorem mk_eq_iff {N : Pic R} : Pic.mk R M = N ↔ Nonempty (M ≃ₗ[R] N) where
453	  mp := (· ▸ ⟨(mk.linearEquiv R M).symm⟩)
454	  mpr := fun ⟨e⟩ ↦ ((equivShrink _).apply_eq_iff_eq_symm_apply).mpr <|
455	    Units.ext <| Quotient.mk_eq_iff_out.mpr ⟨(Finite.reprEquivₛ R M ≪≫ₗ e).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
456	
457	theorem mk_eq_self {M : Pic R} : Pic.mk R M = M := mk_eq_iff.mpr ⟨.refl ..⟩
458	
459	theorem ext_iff {M N : Pic R} : M = N ↔ Nonempty (M ≃ₗ[R] N) := by
460	  rw [← mk_eq_iff, mk_eq_self]
461	
462	theorem mk_eq_mk_iff : Pic.mk R M = Pic.mk R N ↔ Nonempty (M ≃ₗ[R] N) :=
463	  let eN := mk.linearEquiv R N
464	  mk_eq_iff.trans ⟨fun ⟨e⟩ ↦ ⟨e ≪≫ₗ eN⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ↦ ⟨e ≪≫ₗ eN.symm⟩⟩
465	
466	theorem mk_self : Pic.mk R R = 1 :=
467	  congr_arg (equivShrink _) <| Units.ext <| Quotient.sound ⟨(Finite.reprEquivₛ R R).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
468	
469	theorem mk_eq_one_iff : Pic.mk R M = 1 ↔ Nonempty (M ≃ₗ[R] R) := by
470	  rw [← mk_self, mk_eq_mk_iff]
471	
472	theorem mk_eq_one_iff_free : Pic.mk R M = 1 ↔ Free R M :=
473	  mk_eq_one_iff.trans Invertible.free_iff_linearEquiv.symm
474	
475	variable (R M) in
476	theorem mk_eq_one [Free R M] : Pic.mk R M = 1 := mk_eq_one_iff_free.mpr ‹_›
477	
478	instance : Free R (1 : Pic R) := mk_eq_one_iff_free.mp mk_eq_self
479	
480	theorem mk_tensor : Pic.mk R (M ⊗[R] N) = Pic.mk R M * Pic.mk R N :=
481	  congr_arg (equivShrink _) <| Units.ext <| by
482	    simp_rw [Pic.mk, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
483	    refine (Quotient.sound ?_).trans (Skeleton.toSkeleton_tensorObj ..)
484	    exact ⟨(Finite.reprEquivₛ R _ ≪≫ₗ TensorProduct.congr
485	      (Finite.reprEquivₛ R M).symm (Finite.reprEquivₛ R N).symm).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
486	
487	theorem mk_dual : Pic.mk R (Dual R M) = (Pic.mk R M)⁻¹ :=
488	  congr_arg (equivShrink _) <| Units.ext <| by
489	    rw [Pic.mk, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
490	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨(Finite.reprEquivₛ R _ ≪≫ₗ (Finite.reprEquivₛ R _).dualMap).toModuleIsoₛ⟩
491	
492	theorem inv_eq_dual (M : Pic R) : M⁻¹ = Pic.mk R (Dual R M) := by
493	  rw [mk_dual, mk_eq_self]
494	
495	theorem mul_eq_tensor (M N : Pic R) : M * N = Pic.mk R (M ⊗[R] N) := by
496	  rw [mk_tensor, mk_eq_self, mk_eq_self]
497	
498	theorem subsingleton_iffₛ : Subsingleton (Pic R) ↔
499	    ∀ (M : Type u) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M], Module.Invertible R M → Free R M :=
500	  .trans ⟨fun _ M _ _ _ ↦ Subsingleton.elim ..,
501	      fun h ↦ ⟨fun M N ↦ by rw [← mk_eq_self (M := M), ← mk_eq_self (M := N), h, h]⟩⟩ <|
502	    forall₄_congr fun _ _ _ _ ↦ mk_eq_one_iff_free
503	
504	theorem subsingleton_iff {R : Type u} [CommRing R] : Subsingleton (Pic R) ↔
505	    ∀ (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M], Module.Invertible R M → Free R M :=
506	  subsingleton_iffₛ.trans
507	    ⟨fun h M ↦ h M, fun h M ↦ let _ := @Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R; h M⟩
508	
509	instance [Subsingleton (Pic R)] : Free R M :=
510	  have := subsingleton_iffₛ.mp ‹_› (Finite.reprₛ R M) inferInstance
511	  .of_equiv (Finite.reprEquivₛ R M)
512	
513	/- TODO: it's still true that the Picard group of a (commutative) local semiring is trivial;
514	in fact invertible modules over a semiring are Zariski-locally free (but projective module may
515	not be). See Remark 7.10, Example 9.6 and 9.8, and Theorem 11.7 in [BorgerJun2024]. -/
516	instance (R) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] : Subsingleton (Pic R) :=
517	  subsingleton_iff.mpr fun _ _ _ _ ↦ free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing
518	
519	/-- The Picard group of a semilocal ring is trivial. -/
520	instance (R) [CommRing R] [Finite (MaximalSpectrum R)] : Subsingleton (Pic R) :=
521	  subsingleton_iff.mpr fun _ _ _ _ ↦ free_of_flat_of_finrank_eq _ _ 1
522	    fun _ ↦ let _ := @Ideal.Quotient.field; Invertible.finrank_eq_one ..
523	
524	variable (R) (A B : Type*) [CommSemiring A] [CommSemiring B] [Algebra R A]
525	
526	open AlgebraTensorModule in
527	/-- Every `R`-algebra `A` gives rise to a homomorphism between Picard groups of `R` and `A`. -/
528	@[simps] noncomputable def mapAlgebra : Pic R →* Pic A where
529	  toFun M := .mk A (A ⊗[R] M)
530	  map_one' := mk_eq_one_iff.mpr (Invertible.free_iff_linearEquiv.mp inferInstance)
531	  map_mul' _ _ := by
532	    rw [← mk_tensor, mk_eq_mk_iff]
533	    refine ⟨congr (.refl ..) (.symm (mk_eq_iff.mp ?_).some) ≪≫ₗ distribBaseChange R A ..⟩
534	    simp_rw [mk_tensor, mk_eq_self]
535	
536	variable {R A B} [Algebra R B] [Algebra A B] [IsScalarTower R A B]
537	
538	theorem mapAlgebra_mapAlgebra {M : Pic R} : mapAlgebra A B (mapAlgebra R A M) = mapAlgebra R B M :=
539	  mk_eq_mk_iff.mpr ⟨AlgebraTensorModule.congr (.refl ..) (mk.linearEquiv ..) ≪≫ₗ
540	    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange ..⟩
541	
542	theorem mapAlgebra_comp_mapAlgebra : (mapAlgebra A B).comp (mapAlgebra R A) = mapAlgebra R B := by
543	  ext; rw [MonoidHom.comp_apply, mapAlgebra_mapAlgebra]
544	
545	theorem mapAlgebra_self_apply {M : Pic R} : mapAlgebra R R M = M :=
546	  mk_eq_iff.mpr ⟨TensorProduct.lid ..⟩
547	
548	theorem mapAlgebra_self : mapAlgebra R R = .id _ := by ext; exact mapAlgebra_self_apply
549	
550	variable {S T : Type*} [CommSemiring S] [CommSemiring T] (f : R →+* S) (g : S →+* T)
551	
552	/-- Every ring homomorphism between commutative semirings induces a homomorphism between
553	Picard groups. -/
554	noncomputable def mapRingHom : Pic R →* Pic S :=
555	  let := f.toAlgebra; mapAlgebra R S
556	
557	theorem mapRingHom_algebraMap : mapRingHom (algebraMap R A) = mapAlgebra R A := by
558	  rw [mapRingHom, toAlgebra_algebraMap]
559	
560	variable {f g}
561	
562	theorem mapRingHom_comp_mapRingHom :
563	    (mapRingHom g).comp (mapRingHom f) = mapRingHom (g.comp f) := by
564	  algebraize [f, g, g.comp f]
565	  simp_rw [mapRingHom, mapAlgebra_comp_mapAlgebra]
566	
567	theorem mapRingHom_mapRingHom {M : Pic R} :
568	    mapRingHom g (mapRingHom f M) = mapRingHom (g.comp f) M :=
569	  congr($mapRingHom_comp_mapRingHom M)
570	
571	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
572	theorem mapRingHom_id : mapRingHom (.id R) = .id _ := by
573	  rw [mapRingHom, mapAlgebra_self]
574	
575	theorem mapRingHom_id_apply {M : Pic R} : mapRingHom (.id R) M = M :=
576	  congr($mapRingHom_id M)
577	
578	/-- Picard group as a functor from the category of commutative semirings to
579	the category of abelian groups. -/
580	noncomputable def functor : CommSemiRingCat.{u} ⥤ CommGrpCat.{u} where
581	  obj R := .of (Pic R)
582	  map f := CommGrpCat.ofHom (mapRingHom f.hom)
583	  map_id _ := CommGrpCat.Hom.ext mapRingHom_id
584	  map_comp _ _ := CommGrpCat.Hom.ext mapRingHom_comp_mapRingHom.symm
585	
586	end Pic
587	
588	variable (A : Type*) [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
589	
590	/-- The relative Picard group of an `R`-algebra `A`, denoted `Pic(A/R)`,
591	defined to be the kernel of `Pic.mapAlgebra R A`. -/
592	noncomputable def relPic : Subgroup (Pic R) := (Pic.mapAlgebra R A).ker
593	
594	theorem relPic_eq_top [Subsingleton (Pic A)] : relPic R A = ⊤ :=
595	  top_unique fun _ _ ↦ Subsingleton.elim ..
596	
597	end CommRing
598	
599	end PicardGroup

601	namespace Module.Invertible
602	
603	variable (R M : Type*) [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Invertible R M]
604	
605	-- TODO: generalize to CommSemiring by generalizing `CommRing.Pic.instSubsingletonOfIsLocalRing`
606	theorem tensorProductComm_eq_refl : TensorProduct.comm R M M = .refl .. := by
607	  let f (P : Ideal R) [P.IsMaximal] := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap P.primeCompl M
608	  let ff (P : Ideal R) [P.IsMaximal] := TensorProduct.map (f P) (f P)
609	  refine LinearEquiv.toLinearMap_injective <| LinearMap.eq_of_localization_maximal _ ff _ ff _ _
610	    fun P _ ↦ .trans (b := (TensorProduct.comm ..).toLinearMap) ?_ ?_
611	  · apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext P.primeCompl (ff P) (ff P)
612	    ext; dsimp
613	    apply IsLocalizedModule.map_apply
614	  let Rp := Localization P.primeCompl
615	  have ⟨e⟩ := free_iff_linearEquiv.mp (inferInstance : Free Rp (LocalizedModule P.primeCompl M))
616	  have e := e.restrictScalars R
617	  ext x y
618	  refine (congr e e ≪≫ₗ equivOfCompatibleSMul Rp ..).injective ?_
619	  suffices e y ⊗ₜ[Rp] e x = e x ⊗ₜ e y by simpa [equivOfCompatibleSMul]
620	  conv_lhs => rw [← mul_one (e y), ← smul_eq_mul, smul_tmul, smul_eq_mul,
621	    mul_comm, ← smul_eq_mul, ← smul_tmul, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
622	
623	variable {R M} in
624	theorem tmul_comm {m₁ m₂ : M} : m₁ ⊗ₜ[R] m₂ = m₂ ⊗ₜ m₁ :=
625	  DFunLike.congr_fun (tensorProductComm_eq_refl ..) (m₂ ⊗ₜ m₁)
626	
627	end Module.Invertible

629	namespace Submodule
630	
631	open Module Invertible
632	
633	variable {R M A}
634	
635	section Semiring
636	
637	variable [Semiring A] [Algebra R A] [FaithfulSMul R A]
638	
639	open LinearMap in
640	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
641	private theorem projective_units_and_mul'_comp_lTensor_bijective (I : (Submodule R A)ˣ) :
642	    Projective R I ∧ Function.Bijective (mul' R A ∘ₗ I.1.subtype.lTensor A) := by
643	  obtain ⟨T, T', hT, hT', one_mem⟩ := mem_span_mul_finite_of_mem_mul (I.inv_mul ▸ one_le.mp le_rfl)
644	  classical
645	  rw [← Set.image2_mul, ← Finset.coe_image₂, mem_span_finset] at one_mem
646	  set S := T.image₂ (· * ·) T'
647	  obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := one_mem
648	  choose a ha b hb eq using fun i : S ↦ Finset.mem_image₂.mp i.2
649	  let f : I →ₗ[R] S → R := .pi fun i ↦ (LinearEquiv.ofInjective
650	      (Algebra.linearMap R A) (FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective R A)).symm.comp <|
651	    restrict (mulRight R (r i • a i)) fun x hx ↦ by
652	      rw [← one_eq_range, ← I.mul_inv]; exact mul_mem_mul hx (I⁻¹.1.smul_mem _ <| hT <| ha i)
653	  have hf (x : I.1) (i : S) : algebraMap R A (f x i) = x * r i • a i := by
654	    dsimp [f, ← Algebra.linearMap_apply]
655	    exact LinearEquiv.ofInjective_symm_apply ..
656	  let g : (S → R) →ₗ[R] I := .lsum _ _ ℕ fun i ↦ .toSpanSingleton _ _ ⟨b i, hT' <| hb i⟩
657	  have hgf : g ∘ₗ f = .id := LinearMap.ext fun x ↦ Subtype.ext <| by
658	    simp only [g, lsum_apply, comp_apply, sum_apply, toSpanSingleton_apply, proj_apply]
659	    simp_rw [coe_sum, coe_smul, Algebra.smul_def, hf, mul_assoc, ← Finset.mul_sum,
660	      Algebra.smul_mul_assoc, eq, (Finset.sum_coe_sort ..).trans hr.2, mul_one, id_apply]
661	  set m := mul' R A ∘ₗ I.1.subtype.lTensor A
662	  have eq : (piScalarRight R R A S).toLinearMap ∘ₗ f.lTensor A =
663	      (.pi fun i : S ↦ mulRight R (r i • a i)) ∘ₗ m := by
664	    ext; simp [(Algebra.smul_def ..).trans (Algebra.commutes ..), hf, m, mul_assoc]
665	  have := (piScalarRight R R A S).injective.comp <| injective_of_comp_eq_id
666	    (f.lTensor A) (g.lTensor A) <| by rw [← lTensor_comp, hgf, lTensor_id]
667	  rw [← LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, ← coe_comp, eq, coe_comp] at this
668	  refine ⟨.of_split f g hgf, .of_comp this, range_eq_top.mp ?_⟩
669	  rw [show m = mulMap ⊤ I ∘ₗ (topEquiv.symm.rTensor I.1).toLinearMap by ext; rfl, range_comp,
670	    LinearEquiv.range, map_top, mulMap_range, top_mul_eq_top_of_mul_eq_one I.inv_mul]
671	
672	open LinearMap in
673	instance projective_units (I : (Submodule R A)ˣ) : Projective R I :=
674	  (projective_units_and_mul'_comp_lTensor_bijective I).1
675	
676	theorem projective_of_isUnit {I : Submodule R A} (hI : IsUnit I) : Projective R I :=
677	  projective_units hI.unit
678	
679	variable (I J : (Submodule R A)ˣ)
680	
681	set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
682	set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
683	/-- Given two invertible `R`-submodules in an `R`-algebra `A`, the `R`-linear map from
684	`I ⊗[R] J` to `I * J` induced by multiplication is an isomorphism. -/
685	noncomputable def tensorEquivMul : I ⊗[R] J ≃ₗ[R] I * J := by
686	  refine .ofBijective _ ⟨.of_comp (f := Submodule.subtype _) ?_, mulMap'_surjective _ _⟩
687	  convert (projective_units_and_mul'_comp_lTensor_bijective J).2.1.comp
688	    (Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ I.1.subtype_injective)
689	  simp_rw [← LinearMap.coe_comp]
690	  congr 1; ext; rfl
691	
692	/-- Given an invertible `R`-submodule `I` in an `R`-algebra `A`, the `R`-linear map
693	from `I ⊗[R] I⁻¹` to `R` induced by multiplication is an isomorphism. -/
694	noncomputable def tensorInvEquiv : I ⊗[R] ↑I⁻¹ ≃ₗ[R] R :=
695	  tensorEquivMul I _ ≪≫ₗ .ofEq _ _ (I.mul_inv.trans one_eq_range) ≪≫ₗ
696	    .symm (.ofInjective _ (FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective R A))
697	
698	instance : Module.Invertible R I := .left (tensorInvEquiv I)
699	
700	open CommRing Pic
701	
702	variable (R A) in
703	/-- The group homomorphism from the invertible submodules in a faithful algebra over `R` to
704	the Picard group of `R`. See Lemma 2.2 in [RobertsSingh1993]. -/
705	@[simps] noncomputable def unitsToPic : (Submodule R A)ˣ →* Pic R where
706	  toFun I := Pic.mk R I
707	  map_one' := mk_eq_one_iff.mpr
708	    ⟨.ofEq _ _ one_eq_range ≪≫ₗ .symm (.ofInjective _ (FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective R A))⟩
709	  map_mul' I J := by rw [← mk_tensor, mk_eq_mk_iff]; exact ⟨(tensorEquivMul I J).symm⟩
710	
711	/-- The image of an invertible `R`-submodule `I ⊆ A` under `unitsToPic` is isomorphic to `I`. -/
712	noncomputable def unitsToPicEquiv (I : (Submodule R A)ˣ) : unitsToPic R A I ≃ₗ[R] I :=
713	  (mk_eq_iff.mp rfl).some.symm
714	
715	variable (R A)
716	
717	theorem ker_unitsToPic :
718	    (unitsToPic R A).ker = (Units.map (spanSingleton R).toMonoidHom).range := by
719	  ext I; constructor <;> intro h
720	  · have e := (mk_eq_one_iff.mp h).some.symm
721	    have e' := (mk_eq_one_iff.mp (inv_mem h)).some.symm
722	    have h := eq_span_singleton_of_surjective e.surjective
723	    have h' := eq_span_singleton_of_surjective e'.surjective
724	    refine ⟨(isUnit_iff_exists_and_exists.mpr ⟨?_, ?_⟩).unit, Units.ext h.symm⟩
725	    · have : span R {(e 1).1 * e' 1} = 1 := by simpa [span_mul_span] using congr($h * $h').symm
726	      have ⟨r, hr⟩ := span_singleton_eq_one_iff.mp this
727	      exact ⟨e' 1 * algebraMap R A r.inv, by simp [← mul_assoc, hr, ← map_mul]⟩
728	    · have : span R {(e' 1).1 * e 1} = 1 := by simpa [span_mul_span] using congr($h' * $h).symm
729	      have ⟨r, hr⟩ := span_singleton_eq_one_iff.mp this
730	      exact ⟨algebraMap R A r.inv * e' 1, by simp [mul_assoc, hr, ← map_mul]⟩
731	  · obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := h
732	    exact mk_eq_one_iff.mpr ⟨.symm <| (.ofInjective (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R A x) fun _ _ eq ↦

778	    convert (AlgebraTensorModule.congr (.refl ..) (submoduleAlgebraEquiv e) ≪≫ₗ e).bijective

## Summary of Mathlib Search Results

**Key Finding**: Mathlib (commit b80f227, April 2026) **does NOT contain** any Picard scheme / Picard functor / Pic⁰ construction for schemes in the AlgebraicGeometry namespace.

### Search Results by Query:

1. **PicardScheme**, **PicardFunctor**, **Pic0**: No results
2. **Picard**: Only ring-theoretic results (CommRing.Pic)
3. **InvertibleSheaf**, **LineBundle**, **Scheme.Pic**, **Scheme.Picard**: No results
4. **Natural language "Picard scheme of a smooth proper curve"**: Only CommRing.Pic results
5. **Leanfinder "Picard scheme of curves..."**: Returns Scheme, AffineScheme, and WeierstrassCurve definitions but no Pic constructions
6. **Representability**: Found representability infrastructure (IsRepresentable), but no Picard-specific application to schemes

### What Exists:
- **Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup.lean** (lines 17–863): Full theory of `CommRing.Pic R` for commutative rings
  - `Module.Invertible R M`: invertible R-modules
  - `CommRing.Pic R`: group of isomorphism classes
  - `CommRing.Pic.mapAlgebra` and `functor` (CommSemiRingCat → CommGrpCat)
  - Exactness results connecting Picard groups to ideal class groups (domains)
  - TODO at line 59–61 explicitly lists: "Connect to invertible sheaves on Spec R" and "Exhibit isomorphism with sheaf cohomology H¹(Spec R, 𝓞ˣ)"

### Conclusion:
**For schemes**: No Picard scheme, Picard functor, or line bundle constructions exist. The AlgebraicGeometry side lacks both invertible sheaves on schemes and any representable Picard structure. The ring-theoretic Pic must be manually lifted to schemes if needed.

4	
5	**`AlgebraicGeometry.genus` (line 87):** SORRY KEPT.

9	(`CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H`) but not the typeclass instances, the
10	`HasSheafCompose` chain, the `Module k`-structure, or the finite-dimensionality
11	theorem needed to assemble it for the structure sheaf of a smooth proper curve.
12	A constant placeholder (`genus C := 0`) is forbidden by `.archon/PROGRESS.md`
13	and would also force the dimension of `Jacobian C` to be `0`, breaking
14	downstream theorems. The sorry is left in place; informative comments and a
15	commented-out sketch of the intended route are added inside the file so the
16	next iteration has a starting point.
17	
18	## 2. Mathlib gap (per-sorry)
19	
20	### `genus`
21	
22	The mathematical content is $g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$, which would
23	unfold (post Phase A) to roughly:
24	
25	```lean
26	def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
27	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
28	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
29	  Module.finrank k (Sheaf.H (OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf C) 1)
30	```
31	
32	**Closest existing Mathlib declaration:**
33	`CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat → ℕ → Type w'` at
34	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean`, with companion
35	`Sheaf.instAddCommGroupH` providing the `AddCommGroup` instance.
36	
37	**Five-step gap chain to close it honestly** (each step is a single helper
38	lemma / instance; matches Phase A of `STRATEGY.md`):
39	
40	1. **Forget the structure sheaf to `AddCommGrpCat`.** The structure sheaf is
41	   `Scheme.sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat ↥X`, and `TopCat.Sheaf C X` is
42	   *definitionally* `CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) C`
43	   (verified by `rfl` via `lean_run_code`). Mathlib has
44	   `RingCat.hasForgetToAddCommGrp : HasForget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat` and a
45	   forget `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat`, so the composite presheaf
46	   `X.sheaf.1.comp (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)`
47	   type-checks. The missing piece is the instance
48	   `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasSheafCompose
49	     (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)`.
50	
51	2. **`HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat`.** Required by
52	   `Sheaf.H`. Should follow from the general theorem that any
53	   Grothendieck-abelian topology with values in a Grothendieck-abelian target
54	   admits sheafification, but a *direct, no-`sorry`* instance for spaces is not
55	   present.
56	
57	3. **`HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat)`.** The
58	   instance `IsGrothendieckAbelian.hasExt` exists in
59	   `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Abelian/GrothendieckCategory/HasExt.lean`. We then
60	   need `IsGrothendieckAbelian (Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat)`, supplied by
61	   `Sheaf.instIsGrothendieckAbelian` provided `Opens X` is `SmallCategory` —
62	   which it is by Mathlib's universe convention on Opens. The wiring is purely
63	   instance-level but currently unbuilt for this concrete site.
64	
65	4. **`Module k`-structure on `Sheaf.H _ 1`.** The output of `Sheaf.H` carries
66	   only `AddCommGroup`. To take `Module.finrank k`, we need `Module k _`
67	   coming from the $k$-algebra structure on $\Gamma(C, \mathcal O_C)$ induced
68	   by `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`. This requires either (a) doing the
69	   cohomology in `ModuleCat (Γ(C, O_C))` and restricting scalars to `k`, or
70	   (b) defining a $k$-action on `Sheaf.H` via functoriality of `H` in the
71	   sheaf argument. Neither route is engineered in Mathlib.
72	
73	5. **Finite-dimensionality.** Serre's theorem (proper $k$-scheme + coherent
74	   sheaf $\Rightarrow$ each $H^i$ is a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space).
75	   `Module.finrank` is *defined* even without finiteness (it returns `0`), so
76	   the definition type-checks at step 4; this step only matters for proving
77	   downstream theorems (e.g. `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` in
78	   `Jacobian.lean`). Absent in Mathlib.
79	
80	**Coherent cohomology of schemes does not exist in Mathlib b80f227.** The only
81	sheaf-cohomology file in the `AlgebraicGeometry` namespace is
82	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/EllAdicCohomology.lean`, which is the étale
83	$\ell$-adic theory and *not* coherent — repurposing it for $H^1(\mathcal O_C)$
84	is not viable.
85	
86	## 3. Helper lemma proposals
87	
88	The smallest helper lemmas (each $\le 30$ lines once the upstream definitions
89	land) that would unblock `genus`:
90	
91	```lean
92	-- Helper 1 (step 1 of the chain): sheaf condition is preserved by forget₂.
93	instance (X : TopCat) :
94	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasSheafCompose
95	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat AddCommGrpCat ⋙
96	        CategoryTheory.forget₂ AddCommGrpCat AddCommGrpCat) := sorry
97	-- (the actual statement composes through RingCat; written here as a single
98	--  forgetful for brevity)
99	
100	-- Helper 2 (step 2 of the chain).
101	instance (X : TopCat) :
102	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify
103	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
104	      AddCommGrpCat := sorry
105	
106	-- Helper 3 (step 3).  Should be derivable from existing infrastructure
107	-- once the abelian / Grothendieck-abelian instances on Sheaf …
108	-- AddCommGrpCat are propagated.
109	instance (X : TopCat) :
110	    CategoryTheory.HasExt
111	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf
112	        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat) := sorry
113	
114	-- Helper 4 (the main bridge): the structure sheaf of a scheme,
115	-- viewed as a sheaf of abelian groups.
116	noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.structureAddCommGrpSheaf
117	    (X : AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme) :
118	    CategoryTheory.Sheaf
119	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toPresheafedSpace.carrier)
120	      AddCommGrpCat := sorry
121	-- Body once Helper 1 lands:
122	--   ⟨X.sheaf.1.comp (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat),
123	--    Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ _ |>.mpr X.sheaf.2⟩
124	
125	-- Helper 5 (Module k-structure on H¹).
126	noncomputable instance
127	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (.of k))) :
128	    Module k ((AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.structureAddCommGrpSheaf C.left).H 1) :=
129	  sorry
130	-- Construction: action on global sections via `C.hom` makes Γ(C, O_C) a
131	-- k-algebra; this k-action propagates to H¹ by (left-)functoriality of
132	-- `Sheaf.H` in the sheaf argument and the additive structure on
133	-- endomorphism groups.
134	
135	-- Helper 6 (Serre's theorem in the curve case — required only downstream).
136	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.finiteDimensional_H1_OC_of_proper_curve
137	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (.of k)))
138	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
139	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
140	    FiniteDimensional k
141	      ((AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.structureAddCommGrpSheaf C.left).H 1) :=
142	  sorry
143	```
144	
145	With Helpers 1–5 in place, the body of `genus` becomes a one-liner:
146	
147	```lean

149	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
150	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
151	  Module.finrank k ((AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.structureAddCommGrpSheaf C.left).H 1)
152	```
153	
154	**Recommended next-iteration target (smallest unit of progress):** Helper 4
155	(`Scheme.structureAddCommGrpSheaf`), gated by Helpers 1–3. Helper 4 is the
156	single declaration whose introduction collapses the bulk of the API gap and

1	# AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean — Discovery report (iteration 0)
2	
3	**Status:** IN PROGRESS — no sorry honestly closed; full gap report below.
4	**Compilation:** file unchanged from autoformalize stage; three `sorry` warnings, zero errors (verified via `lean_diagnostic_messages`).
5	**Blueprint:** `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelJacobi.tex` already exists with the informal proof sketch (Definition `def:ofCurve`, Lemma `lem:comp_ofCurve`, Theorem `thm:exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`). No new chapter needed.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Sorry status
10	
11	No sorry was closed. All three (`ofCurve` L34, `comp_ofCurve` L40, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` L49) reduce, by the blueprint, to a definition / theorem about the still-`sorry` constant `Jacobian C`. They are **structurally blocked** by `Jacobian.lean`, plus an additional independent gap (rigidity for morphisms of abelian varieties) needed for uniqueness.
12	
13	Critically, **none of the forbidden shortcut definitions can be honestly closed downstream**:
14	
15	- *Constant map.* Setting `ofCurve P := toUnit C ≫ η[Jacobian C]` (the constant map to the identity element) makes `comp_ofCurve` provable trivially, but then for `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` with `A = Jacobian C` (assume already constructed) and `f` a non-constant pointed morphism `C ⟶ Jacobian C`, the equation `f = ofCurve P ≫ g = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) ≫ g = toUnit C ≫ η[A]` forces `f` to be constant — contradicting the existence of any non-constant `f`. Such non-constant `f` exist whenever `genus C ≥ 1` (e.g. `f = id_{Jacobian C}` after composing `C → Jacobian C` with a non-trivial endomorphism, witnessed by a non-trivial line bundle of degree 0). So the theorem becomes *false*, hence unprovable. Therefore the constant-map definition is mathematically forbidden, not merely an "ugly" placeholder.
16	- *Identity-map.* `ofCurve P := 𝟙 C` is type-incorrect: target is `Jacobian C ≠ C`.
17	- *Pull from terminal scheme.* If `Jacobian C` were defined as `𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` (forbidden by Jacobian.lean's plan), `ofCurve` would coincide with `toUnit C` and the same falsehood would follow.
18	
19	Conclusion (per blueprint discovery objective 1): **`ofCurve` cannot be defined without first having a substantive construction of `Jacobian C`** (the Picard-zero scheme). This is the mathematical content recorded explicitly.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 2. Mathlib gap (per sorry)
24	
25	### 2.1 `ofCurve` (line 34)
26	
27	**Type goal.** `(P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) → (C ⟶ Jacobian C)`.
28	
29	**What is needed mathematically.** A morphism `C → Pic⁰_{C/k}` representing the line bundle `O_{C×C}(Δ - p₁* P)` on `C × C`, normalized so that the fiber over `P` is trivial. Equivalently: the universal property of the Picard scheme as a moduli of degree-zero line bundles, applied to `O(Q - P)`.
30	
31	**Closest Mathlib declaration.** None for schemes.
32	- `CommRing.Pic` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean`) — Picard *group of a ring*, not the *Picard scheme* of a scheme; no group-scheme structure, no representability statement.
33	- No `Scheme.Pic`, `PicardScheme`, `Pic0`, `LineBundle X`, or `InvertibleSheaf X` exists (verified by `lean_local_search` on `Pic`, `Picard`, `LineBundle`, `InvertibleSheaf` — all returned 0 hits except `CommRing.Pic`).
34	
35	**API gap.** No notion of (a) a quasi-coherent sheaf on a scheme that is invertible, (b) the relative Picard *functor* `(Sch/k)ᵒᵖ → Ab`, (c) its representability by a scheme `Pic_{C/k}`, (d) the connected component of the identity `Pic⁰_{C/k}`. Without these, `ofCurve` has no honest construction.
36	
37	### 2.2 `comp_ofCurve` (line 40)
38	
39	**Type goal.** `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]`.
40	
41	**What is needed mathematically.** That the universal morphism `ofCurve P` sends `P` to the identity of the group scheme. Mathematically: the line bundle `O_{C×C}(Δ - p₁* P)` restricted along `(P, id)` is `O_C(P - P) = O_C`, classifying the trivial point of `Pic⁰`.
42	
43	**Closest Mathlib declaration.** None for schemes.
44	
45	**API gap.** Conditional on `ofCurve` being defined via the universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (as in 2.1), this lemma reduces to: the restriction of `O_{C×C}(Δ - p₁* P)` along the section `(P, id) : C ⟶ C × C` is the trivial line bundle. That requires:
46	- pullback functoriality of invertible sheaves;
47	- `O(D - D) = O` (cancellation in the divisor group, equivalently triviality of the trivial divisor);
48	- the recipe sending a "normalized line bundle on C × T" to a morphism `T ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the universal property again).
49	
50	All three are part of the Picard-scheme infrastructure. Independent obstacle: the way `η[Jacobian C]` is encoded (as `MonObj.one : 𝟙_ ⟶ Jacobian C` from the `GrpObj` instance) demands that `Jacobian.instGrpObj` already chose the *correct* group structure compatible with `Pic⁰`.
51	
52	### 2.3 `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (line 49)
53	
54	**Type goal.** `∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g`, given `f : C ⟶ A`, `P ≫ f = η[A]`, with `A` an abelian variety (smooth + proper + GeometricallyIrreducible + GrpObj).
55	
56	**What is needed mathematically (blueprint §Universal property).**
57	- *Existence:* dual abelian variety `A^∨`, biduality `A^{∨∨} ≃ A`, and the Picard-functoriality of `A` that classifies the line bundle on `C × A^∨` corresponding to `f` ↦ a `k`-morphism `A^∨ ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k} = Jacobian C`, dualised.
58	- *Uniqueness:* the **rigidity theorem for morphisms of abelian varieties** (Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, §4 Theorem 1): a morphism `g : X ⟶ Y` of abelian varieties that vanishes on a non-empty open subset is the constant zero map. Combined with the fact that `image(ofCurve P)` generates `Jacobian C` (via symmetric powers `C^{(g)} ↠ Jacobian C`).
59	
60	**Closest Mathlib declaration.** `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean`:
Let me check Genus.lean to see how the other prover added comments to their file.

/-!
# The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve

The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
its structure as an abelian variety.

## Status (iteration 001 — discovery)

Mathlib b80f227 has **no Picard scheme, Picard functor, or `Pic⁰` construction
for schemes**: only `CommRing.Pic R` (Picard group of a commutative ring,
`Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean`) and the abstract `GrpObj` API exist.
There is also no formal `AbelianVariety` typeclass — abelian varieties are
encoded ad hoc by combining `[GrpObj] [IsProper] [GeometricallyIrreducible]`.

All five `sorry`s below (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`,
`instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) are corollaries of a **single**
representability theorem that is missing from Mathlib:

> *(Pic-representable.)* For a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve
> `C` over a field `k`, the étale-sheafified Picard functor of `C/k` is
> representable by a `k`-group scheme `Pic_{C/k}` whose connected component of
> the identity `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible
> abelian variety of relative dimension `genus C` over `Spec k`.

This is Phase C of `STRATEGY.md`. See `.archon/task_results/Jacobian.md` for
the per-`sorry` gap analysis, the proposed helper lemmas (with full Lean
signatures), and the search trail that confirms the absence of the API.

### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)

Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` (the terminal object,
i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free:

* `instGrpObj` via `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ _)`,
* `instIsProper` since `𝟙 (Spec k)` is proper,
* `instGeometricallyIrreducible` since `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.

But the fourth instance,
`SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom`, becomes
`SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙 (Spec (.of k)))`. The identity is smooth
of relative dimension `0`, so the only honest proof would force `genus C = 0`,
which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object
definition is therefore forbidden by the plan-agent rules and not used here.
-/

# AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean — iteration 001 (discovery)

**Status:** IN PROGRESS — no sorry honestly closed; full gap report below.
**Compilation:** five `sorry` warnings, zero errors (verified via `lean_diagnostic_messages` after edits).
**Blueprint:** `blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex` already exists with the
informal proof sketch (`def:Jacobian`, `thm:Jacobian_grpObj`,
`thm:Jacobian_smooth_genus`, `thm:Jacobian_proper`, `thm:Jacobian_geomIrred`).
No new chapter created. *(Note: the prover prompt referenced
`AlgebraicJacobian_Jacobian.tex`, but the existing chapter file is
`Jacobian.tex` — content is consistent with this report.)*

---

## 1. Sorry status

No sorry was closed. All five sorries (`Jacobian` L31, `instGrpObj` L40,
`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` L45, `instIsProper` L49,
`instGeometricallyIrreducible` L53 in the original file; line numbers shifted
after adding the discovery docstring and per-sorry comment blocks) are
**simultaneously blocked** by the absence of any Picard scheme / Picard functor
/ `Pic⁰` construction for schemes in Mathlib b80f227.

**Crucially, the four instances are not independent — they all reduce to a
single representability theorem** (Theorem `thm:Pic_representable` in the
blueprint, FGA / Mumford §III.13):

> For a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
> the étale-sheafified Picard functor of `C/k` is representable by a `k`-group
> scheme `Pic_{C/k}` whose connected component of the identity `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
> a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative
> dimension `genus C`, hence an abelian variety.

Once `Pic⁰_{C/k}` exists with this bundle of properties, the four instances
are one-line corollaries: pick the connected component, transport the group
structure, transport smoothness/properness/irreducibility, identify dimension
with `genus C`. That is the structural confirmation requested by
`.archon/PROGRESS.md` objective 2.iii.

### Forbidden shortcut — sanity check on `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))`

(Recorded explicitly per `.archon/PROGRESS.md` objective 2 and the forbidden
shortcuts in `task_pending.md`.)

The terminal object of `Over (Spec (.of k))` is `Spec (.of k)` itself, equipped
with the identity morphism to `Spec (.of k)`. Setting `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _`
discharges three of the four instances trivially:

* `instGrpObj`: provided by `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ C)` (Mathlib).
* `instIsProper`: `𝟙 (Spec (.of k))` is proper (every closed immersion / every
  identity is proper).
* `instGeometricallyIrreducible`: base change of `Spec k` to any extension is
  `Spec` of that extension, an integral one-point scheme, hence irreducible.

**The fourth instance fails honestly.** The goal becomes
`SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom`, i.e.
`SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙 (Spec (.of k)))`. The identity
morphism on any scheme is smooth of relative dimension `0`, and `0` is the
unique witness — there is no honest way to give it any other relative
dimension. The closure obligation therefore reduces to `genus C = 0`, which is
mathematically *false* whenever `C` has positive genus (e.g. an elliptic
curve). Any "proof" closing the obligation would have to falsely identify
`genus C` with `0`. Hence the terminal-object definition is forbidden not for
stylistic reasons but because it makes `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`
unprovable in genus `≥ 1`.

The same kind of obstruction rules out every other constant-scheme candidate
(`𝔸¹_k`, `Spec L` for an extension `L/k`, …): each would force `genus C` to
match the relative dimension of the chosen scheme, contradicting the
definition of `genus` as `dim_k H¹(C, O_C)`.

## 2. Mathlib gap (per sorry)

### 2.1 `Jacobian` (L31)

**Type goal.** `Over (Spec (.of k))`, given `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
[IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.

**Mathematical content (blueprint `def:Jacobian`).**
`Jac(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k}`, viewed as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.

**Closest Mathlib declaration.** `CommRing.Pic R` in
`Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean` — Picard *group* of a commutative ring
(an abelian group, not a scheme), with basic theory (Mayer–Vietoris-style
`Pic.mapAlgebra`, the functor `CommSemiRingCat ⥤ CommGrpCat`, vanishing for
local rings, exactness with the ideal class group). `lean_loogle "CommRing.Pic"`
returned 8 hits, all confined to that file.

**API gap (verified by `lean_local_search`):** every one of `PicardScheme`,
`PicardFunctor`, `Pic0`, `Scheme.Pic`, `Scheme.Picard`, `LineBundle`,
`InvertibleSheaf` returned **0 hits**. There is therefore no API for:
1. The Picard functor `(Sch/k)ᵒᵖ → Ab`, `S ↦ Pic(C ×_k S)/Pic(S)`.
2. Étale sheafification of group-valued functors on `Sch/k`.
3. Representability of the sheafified Picard functor by a smooth, locally
   finite-type `k`-group scheme.
4. The connected-component-of-identity construction for group schemes locally
   of finite type over a field (no `GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity`
   in Mathlib).
5. Invertible-sheaf / line-bundle theory on schemes (the entire scheme-side
   line-bundle dictionary is absent).

### 2.2 `instGrpObj` (L40)

**Type goal.** `GrpObj (Jacobian C)` (which is `GrpObj (Pic⁰_{C/k})` once 2.1
lands).

**Mathematical content.** The group law on `Pic⁰_{C/k}` induced by tensor
product of line bundles on `C × T`, compatible with base change in `T`.
Constructed as part of the same representability theorem.

**Closest Mathlib declaration.** None for *Picard*-style group schemes. For
abstract group-object structure on schemes, Mathlib has
`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean`:
* `AlgebraicGeometry.isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_isIntegral_tensorObj_of_isAlgClosed`
  — proper + geometrically integral group scheme is commutative (Stacks 0BFD).
This proves `Pic⁰_{C/k}` will be commutative *after* it has `GrpObj`, but does
not provide the `GrpObj` itself.

**API gap.** Same as 2.1: without the Picard functor and its representability,
the group law on `Pic⁰` cannot be exhibited. The proof of `instGrpObj` once
representability is in is essentially: transport the group law from the
representable functor `Pic⁰` to the representing scheme, by Yoneda.

### 2.3 `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (L45)

**Type goal.** `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom`.

**Mathematical content (blueprint `thm:Jacobian_smooth_genus`).** The tangent
space of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` at the identity is `H¹(C, O_C)`, hence has `k`-dimension
`genus C` by definition. Smoothness then propagates everywhere by translation
(group scheme), giving smoothness of relative dimension `genus C`.

**Closest Mathlib declaration.**
* `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension`
  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean`) — the typeclass we need
  to populate.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth` — the projection to
  plain `Smooth`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`
  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Smooth.lean`) — useful but only proves
  *smoothness* (no relative dimension), and only over an algebraically closed
  base; relative dimension is the missing piece.

**API gap.**
1. The tangent space of a group scheme at the identity (`Lie(G)` in classical
   notation) is absent — Mathlib has no
   `GrpObj.tangentSpaceAtIdentity : GrpObj G → ModuleCat k`.
2. The identification `Lie(Pic⁰_{C/k}) ≃ H¹(C, O_C)` (a special case of the
   deformation theory of line bundles, Hartshorne III §12 or Mumford
   *Abelian Varieties* §III.13) requires both the Picard scheme and scheme
   cohomology — neither exists.
3. The propagation "dimension at identity = relative dimension everywhere" for
   smooth group schemes over a field requires translation invariance + a
   smoothness criterion in terms of fiber dimension, also currently absent
   from Mathlib for schemes.

### 2.4 `instIsProper` (L49)

**Type goal.** `IsProper (Jacobian C).hom`.

**Mathematical content (blueprint `thm:Jacobian_proper`).** `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
proper because `Pic_{C/k}` is proper *for curves* (a special feature of the
curve case via the Abel–Jacobi theorem / boundedness of degree-zero divisor
classes), and a connected component of a proper scheme is proper.

**Closest Mathlib declaration.** `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`
(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`) — the typeclass to
populate. No general "connected component of a proper scheme is proper" lemma.

**API gap.** As above (2.1): without `Pic_{C/k}` to start from, properness
has nothing to inherit from. Independently we would also need:
* `IsProper.connectedComponentOfIdentity` — properness restricts to
  components, which Mathlib has not proved at the scheme level.

### 2.5 `instGeometricallyIrreducible` (L53)

**Type goal.** `GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom`.

**Mathematical content (blueprint `thm:Jacobian_geomIrred`).** `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
geometrically irreducible because (a) it is the connected component of the
identity in a *smooth* group scheme, hence connected smooth, hence irreducible
(connected smooth schemes are irreducible because smooth implies normal
implies locally irreducible, and connected + locally irreducible gives
irreducibility); (b) connectedness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is preserved by base change
to `\bar k` because `Pic⁰` commutes with extensions of the base field.

**Closest Mathlib declaration.** `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`
(typeclass to populate). No "connected smooth group scheme is geometrically
irreducible" lemma.

**API gap.** Beyond 2.1: Mathlib lacks the chain
*connected ⇒ irreducible for smooth group schemes over a field* and the
base-change compatibility of `Pic⁰`.

## 3. Helper lemma proposals

The five sorries collapse to **one large infrastructure target** — the Picard
scheme of a curve — plus a handful of *general* group-scheme lemmas that have
independent value. Listed in increasing scope.

### 3.1 The single bundled helper (collapses 2.1–2.5 simultaneously)

```lean
-- Phase C: the representability theorem for the Picard functor of a smooth
-- proper geometrically irreducible curve, packaged as the existence of an
-- abelian-variety-shaped object.
theorem AlgebraicGeometry.exists_jacobian_scheme
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    ∃ (J : Over (Spec (.of k))) (_ : GrpObj J)
      (_ : IsProper J.hom) (_ : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom),
        SmoothOfRelativeDimension (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C) J.hom := sorry
```

With this single theorem, each sorry becomes a 1-liner — pull out
`(exists_jacobian_scheme C).choose` for `Jacobian` and the corresponding
component of `.choose_spec` for the four instances. **However**, this is not
the right helper to actually prove: it bundles the *witness* (the scheme `J`)
with its *properties*, so other downstream theorems (notably `ofCurve` and
`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) cannot use a structural description of `J`.
They would need a definitional unfolding of `J` to carry out moduli arguments
— which `.choose` blocks.

The real target is therefore the next decomposition.

### 3.2 The structurally honest decomposition (Phase C of `STRATEGY.md`)

Step 1 (line bundles on schemes; Phase B of `STRATEGY.md` overlaps).

```lean
-- A line bundle on a scheme is an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module.
def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle (X : Scheme) : Type _ := sorry
-- Instance: this forms a commutative group under tensor product (Pic group).
noncomputable instance (X : Scheme) :
    CommGroup (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle X) := sorry
-- The total Picard group of a scheme.
def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic (X : Scheme) : Type _ :=
  AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle X
-- Pull-back functoriality.
def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic.pullback {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic Y →* AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic X := sorry
```

Step 2 (Picard functor of `C/k`).

```lean
/- The Picard functor of `C` over `k`: `S ↦ Pic(C ×_k S)/Pic(S)`. -/
noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.PicardFunctor
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) :
    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := sorry
```

Step 3 (étale sheafification — uses Mathlib's existing
`CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.HasSheafify` for the étale site once the
étale Grothendieck topology on `Sch/k` is in place; partially in
`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/`).

```lean
/- The Picard sheaf, étale sheafification of the Picard functor. -/
noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.PicardSheaf
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) :
    Sheaf (etaleTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) AddCommGrpCat.{u} := sorry
```

Step 4 (representability — the heart of FGA / Mumford §III.13).

```lean
/- The Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. -/
noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) := sorry
/- It represents the Picard sheaf. -/
theorem AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme.represents
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    yoneda.obj (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme C) ≅
      (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardSheaf C).val := sorry
```

Step 5 (connected component of identity for group schemes).

```lean
/- The connected component of the identity of a locally-of-finite-type
   group scheme over a field. -/
noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G]
    [LocallyOfFinitePresentation G.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) := sorry
/- It carries an inherited GrpObj structure. -/
noncomputable instance
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G]
    [LocallyOfFinitePresentation G.hom] :
    GrpObj (AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity G) :=
  sorry
```

Step 6 (the Jacobian itself). With Steps 1–5 in place, the body becomes:

```lean
def AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
  AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity
    (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme C)
```

and the `instGrpObj` is the inherited group structure from Step 5 on
`PicardScheme C`'s identity component, applied via `instance` resolution.

Step 7 (properness of `Pic_{C/k}` for the curve case). Curve-specific.

```lean
instance AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme.isProper
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    IsProper (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme C).hom := sorry
-- `Pic⁰` inherits properness from `Pic`.
instance AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity.isProper
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G]
    [LocallyOfFinitePresentation G.hom] [IsProper G.hom] :
    IsProper
      (AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity G).hom :=
  sorry
```

Step 8 (smoothness of `Pic_{C/k}` and dimension formula).

```lean
-- Smoothness of the Picard scheme of a curve (Hartshorne III §12).
instance AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme.smooth
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    Smooth (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme C).hom := sorry
-- The dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `genus C`.
theorem AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity.dim_eq_genus
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
    SmoothOfRelativeDimension (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C)
      (AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity
        (AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme C)).hom := sorry
```

Step 9 (geometric irreducibility — connected smooth ⇒ irreducible, then
`Pic⁰` commutes with field extension).

```lean
-- A connected smooth `k`-group scheme is geometrically irreducible.
instance
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G]
    [Smooth G.hom] [Connected G.left] :
    GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom := sorry
```

Step 10 (the four instance bodies). With Steps 1–9:

```lean
instance AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := inferInstance
instance AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus :
    SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
  AlgebraicGeometry.PicardScheme.connectedComponentOfIdentity.dim_eq_genus C
instance AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instIsProper :
    IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := inferInstance
instance AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGeometricallyIrreducible :
    GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom := inferInstance
```

All four are ≤ 1 line, well under the 20-line budget set by the discovery
directive.

### 3.3 Phase classification (per `.archon/STRATEGY.md`)

| Sorry | Helper steps | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| `Jacobian` (2.1) | Steps 1–6 | C |
| `instGrpObj` (2.2) | Steps 1–6 (the GrpObj inheritance is part of Step 5/6) | C |
| `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (2.3) | Steps 1–8 (uses Phase A cohomology + Phase C dimension) | A + C |
| `instIsProper` (2.4) | Steps 1–7 | C |
| `instGeometricallyIrreducible` (2.5) | Steps 1–6 + Step 9 | C |

The 4 instances therefore *all* reduce to corollaries of a single underlying
representability theorem (Steps 1–6 + Step 7/8/9 = the bundle of properties of
`Pic⁰_{C/k}`), confirming the structural claim required by
`.archon/PROGRESS.md` objective 2.iii.

### 3.4 Recommended next-iteration target (smallest unit of progress)

**Step 1 first** — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle` and its commutative
group structure under tensor product. This is the smallest *self-contained*
helper (no scheme-cohomology dependency, only quasi-coherent-sheaf machinery
which Mathlib has via `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules`). It seeds Steps 2–6.
Independent of Phase A (cohomology) and proceeds in parallel with the Genus
file's Phase A targets.

The next iteration's refactor directive should likely create
`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean` with the `LineBundle` definition
and basic API (tensor product, dual, pull-back), and a corresponding blueprint
chapter `Picard.tex` extracted from §3.1 of `Jacobian.tex`.
